not_cf_wt_noninterferent_com.solution:
Example not_cf_wt_noninterferent_com :
  ~ xpub |-cf- <{ if Y=0
                 then Z := 0
                 else skip
                 end }>.
Proof.
  apply cf_typechecker_complete. reflexivity.
Qed.
not_cf_wt_noninterferent_com.explanation: Use the type-checker completeness to show the program is not cf-well-typed: the checker returns false because Y is secret.

ts_typechecker.solution:
Fixpoint ts_typechecker (P:pub_vars) (pc:label) (c:com) : bool :=
  match c with
  | <{ skip }> => true
  | <{ X := a }> => can_flow (join pc (label_of_aexp P a)) (P X)
  | <{ c1 ; c2 }> => ts_typechecker P pc c1 && ts_typechecker P pc c2
  | <{ if b then c1 else c2 end }> =>
      ts_typechecker P (join pc (label_of_bexp P b)) c1 &&
      ts_typechecker P (join pc (label_of_bexp P b)) c2
  | <{ while b do c1 end }> =>
      can_flow (join pc (label_of_bexp P b)) public &&
      ts_typechecker P (join pc (label_of_bexp P b)) c1
  end.
ts_typechecker.explanation: The ts_typechecker adds a termination-sensitivity check for while loops: the join of pc and the guard label must flow to public, ensuring the guard doesn't depend on secrets.

ts_typechecker_sound.explanation: Soundness of the ts type-checker: if it returns true, the program is well-typed. By induction on the command.

ts_typechecker_complete.solution:
Lemma ts_typechecker_complete : forall P pc c,
  ts_typechecker P pc c = false ->
  ~ P ;; pc |-ts- c.
Proof.
  intros P pc c H Hc. induction Hc; simpl in *;
    try rewrite andb_false_iff in *; try tauto; try congruence.
  - apply label_of_aexp_unique in H0. rewrite H0 in *. congruence.
  - destruct H; apply label_of_bexp_unique in H0; subst; eauto.
  - rewrite andb_false_iff in H. destruct H.
    + apply label_of_bexp_unique in H0. subst. congruence.
    + apply label_of_bexp_unique in H0. subst. auto.
Qed.
ts_typechecker_complete.explanation: Completeness: if the checker returns false, the program is not well-typed.

not_ts_non_termination_com.solution:
Example not_ts_non_termination_com :
  ~ xpub ;; public |-ts- termination_leak.
Proof.
  apply ts_typechecker_complete. reflexivity.
Qed.
not_ts_non_termination_com.explanation: The termination_leak program branches on secret Y in a while guard, so it is rejected by the termination-sensitive type system.

cf_well_typed_ts_cf_secure.solution:
Theorem cf_well_typed_ts_cf_secure : forall P c,
  P |-cf- c ->
  ts_cf_secure P c.
Proof.
  intros P c Hwt. induction Hwt; unfold ts_cf_secure; intros.
  - (* skip *) inversion H0; subst. exists s2. constructor. auto.
  - (* asgn *) inversion H1; subst. exists (x !-> aeval s2 a ; s2).
    constructor. auto.
  - (* seq *) inversion H1; subst.
    edestruct IHHwt1 as [s2' Hs2']; eauto.
    edestruct IHHwt2 as [s2'' Hs2'']; eauto.
    eapply cf_well_typed_noninterferent; eauto.
    exists s2''. econstructor; eauto.
  - (* if *) inversion H2; subst.
    + assert (beval s2 b = true) as Hb2.
      { eapply noninterferent_bexp; eauto. apply label_of_bexp_sound.
        rewrite (label_of_bexp_unique _ _ _ H). auto. }
      edestruct IHHwt1 as [s2' Hs2']; eauto.
      exists s2'. apply CFE_IfTrue; auto.
    + assert (beval s2 b = false) as Hb2.
      { eapply noninterferent_bexp; eauto. apply label_of_bexp_sound.
        rewrite (label_of_bexp_unique _ _ _ H). auto. }
      edestruct IHHwt2 as [s2' Hs2']; eauto.
      exists s2'. apply CFE_IfFalse; auto.
  - (* while *) inversion H1; subst.
    assert (beval s2 b = beval s1 b) as Hb.
    { symmetry. eapply noninterferent_bexp; eauto. apply label_of_bexp_sound.
      rewrite (label_of_bexp_unique _ _ _ H). auto. }
    rewrite <- H7 in Hb. destruct (beval s1 b) eqn:Eb.
    + edestruct IHHwt as [s2' Hs2']; eauto.
      { eapply cf_well_typed_noninterferent; eauto. }
      exists s2'. econstructor; eauto. rewrite Hb. auto.
    + exists s2. constructor. rewrite Hb. auto.
Qed.
cf_well_typed_ts_cf_secure.explanation: cf-well-typed programs are ts_cf_secure: both executions follow the same control flow because guards only depend on public state.

public_outputs.solution:
(* The output typing rule:
  | ONIWT_Output : forall pc a l,
      P |-a- a \in l ->
      can_flow (join pc l) public = true ->
      P ;; pc |-ni- output a

The oni_typechecker case for output:
  | output a => can_flow (join pc (label_of_aexp P a)) public

The oni_typechecker_sound case:
  Output case follows from label_of_aexp_sound and the can_flow check.

The oni_typechecker_complete case:
  Output case: use label_of_aexp_unique to derive contradiction.

not_ni_wt_output1:
  apply oni_typechecker_complete. reflexivity.

not_ni_wt_output2:
  apply oni_typechecker_complete. reflexivity.

weaken_pc for output:
  Apply ONIWT_Output with can_flow_join_l and can_flow_trans.

secret_run_no_output:
  By induction on evaluation. Assignment and skip produce no output.
  Sequence and if cases use IH. The output case contradicts
  the typing: join secret l cannot flow to public.

oni_well_typed_noninterferent:
  Extend the ni_well_typed proof with:
  - Output case: use noninterferent_aexp to show output values are equal.
  - If/While cases: use different_code for secret branches,
    different_code_no_output for output equality.

oni_well_typed_output_secure:
  Similar to noninterferent but additionally track output equality
  using secret_run_no_output for secret branches.
*)
public_outputs.explanation: Extension of the type system with public outputs. The key additions are: typing rule requiring output expressions to flow to public, type-checker case, soundness/completeness cases, and security proofs tracking output equality.
